hero.alt: Walrus with long white tusks resting on Arctic ground in Svalbard
hero.caption: Walruses are huge Arctic sea mammals known for their long tusks and bristly whiskers.

habitat.alt: Walruses hauled out on floating sea ice in the Arctic
habitat.caption: Walruses live in the cold Arctic, resting on floating sea ice and rocky coasts.

diet.fileTitle: File:Close up of head of young bull walrus marine mammal in water odobenus rosmarus.jpg
diet.alt: Close-up of a walrus head showing thick whiskers above the water
diet.caption: Walruses use their sensitive whiskers to feel for clams and shellfish on the dark seabed.

baby.fileTitle: File:Walrus cow with her young calf on the ice odobenus rosmarus.jpg
baby.alt: Mother walrus resting on ice beside her young calf
baby.caption: A walrus calf stays close to its mother for two years or more while it grows.

family.fileTitle: File:Walrus (Odobenus rosmarus) resting in a crowd on Wahlbergøya, Svalbard (2).jpg
family.alt: Crowd of walruses piled together resting on a beach in Svalbard
family.caption: Walruses are very social and crowd together in big groups called herds when they rest.

range.fileTitle: File:Pacific walrus surfacing through ice on the Alaska coast.jpg
range.alt: Walrus surfacing through a gap in the sea ice off Alaska
range.caption: Walruses live around the icy seas of the Arctic, near Alaska, Canada, Russia, and Norway.

size.alt: Full body view of a massive male walrus on a beach
size.caption: Big male walruses can weigh over a ton, as much as a small car.

closeup.fileTitle: File:A Face Only a Mother Could Love? (13958342330).jpg
closeup.alt: Close-up of a walrus face with tusks and dense whiskers
closeup.caption: A walrus has hundreds of stiff whiskers and two long tusks that never stop growing.

fun-fact.fileTitle: File:Pacific walrus bull animal hauled out.jpg
fun-fact.alt: Walrus using its tusks while hauling its body onto land
fun-fact.caption: A walrus can hook its tusks into the ice to drag itself out of the water, like tooth-walking.

core-habitat.alt: Walrus resting near an Arctic glacier and cold water
core-habitat.caption: Walruses need shallow Arctic seas for feeding and ice or shore for resting.

core-diet.alt: Walrus swimming underwater in cold Arctic seas
core-diet.caption: Walruses dive to the seafloor to search for food and can eat thousands of clams in one meal.

core-baby.fileTitle: File:A curious pacific walrus calf checks out the photographer from the icy water.jpg
core-baby.alt: Young walrus calf peeking out of the icy water
core-baby.caption: Calves can swim soon after birth and ride on their mother's back when they get tired.

core-family.fileTitle: File:Walrus (Odobenus rosmarus) resting in a crowd on Wahlbergøya, Svalbard (4).jpg
core-family.alt: Many walruses huddled together on an Arctic shore
core-family.caption: Huddling together keeps walruses warm and helps protect the young in the middle of the herd.

core-range.fileTitle: File:Pacific walrus hauled out on a round island beach.jpg
core-range.alt: Walruses hauled out on a rocky Arctic island beach
core-range.caption: When sea ice melts, walruses gather on coastal beaches across the Arctic to rest.
